mnemonics: ISA FITS All Needs: Instruction Set Architecture Formats Include Types and Sequencing
OPCODE Your Way: Operands, Purpose, Cycles, Dataflow, Execution
keyPoints: An instruction consists of an opcode and operands, specifying the operation and its data.
The fetch-decode-execute cycle is the fundamental sequence for processing a single instruction.
Control signals generated during decoding direct the datapath components (ALUs, registers, buses) to perform the operation.
commonMistakes: Confusing the ISA (architecture/interface) with the microarchitecture (specific implementation).
Assuming all instructions take the same number of clock cycles to execute (e.g., multiply vs. add).
